problem_structure.unknown: the empty niche in the center of the grid — the carved wooden bird that should be there but is not
data: twenty-five shallow stone niches arranged in five rows and five columns
twenty-four carved wooden birds of different sizes, shapes, and woods
one empty niche in the central position of the third row
problem_structure.condition: the gate opens only when the solver names what is missing, not when the present birds are arranged, categorised, or catalogued

correct_approach: Yejide stopped searching among the birds, sat down, looked at the grid as a structure rather than a collection of items, and asked what should occupy the empty niche; naming the absence caused the door to unlock before she moved.
